name: prettier_fmt
description: Format or check code formatting with Prettier in one call. Supports --write (format in-place) and --check (verify formatting) actions. Targets can be specific files, directories, or glob patterns. Replaces 'cd /app && npx prettier --write/--check <files>' boilerplate.
when_to_use: Use when you need to format TypeScript/JavaScript/CSS/MD files with Prettier after making changes, or check if files are properly formatted before committing. Use --write to auto-format files, --check to verify formatting (e.g., before running tsc or committing). Target specific files or use glob patterns like 'src/**/*.ts' to format all TypeScript files.

parameters.name: targets
parameters.description: Space-separated file paths, directories, or glob patterns to format/check (e.g., 'src/**/*.ts' or 'src/schema/lazy/schema_.ts src/schema/lazy/schema_.unit.test.ts')

parameters.name: --no-cd
parameters.description: Stay in current directory instead of cd'ing to project_root

examples.expected: Formats a single TypeScript file in-place.
Checks formatting of all TypeScript files.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2 steps (cd to project, npx prettier command) with a single script call. Eliminates repetitive 'cd /app && npx prettier --write/--check ...' boilerplate that appears multiple times per trajectory in TypeScript projects. The --check action with exit code handling replaces separate formatting verification steps.
